terms.notes: First- and third-person forms merge; the third-person form is used for first person. Also 'our (same sex) sibling'.
mother's male parallel cousin; male's father's mother's brother's daughter
male's parallel female cousin
male's parallel male cousin; female's parallel female cousin; father's mother's brother's child (of same sex as speaker)
female's parallel male cousin; female's father's mother's brother's son
brother's grandchild; sister's grandchild; mother's brother's grandchild
generally used for males marrying into a family; (ut...)

terms.english_term: to have as a female in-law
terms.notes: e.g., mother-, sister-, daughter-in-law; (ir...)

terms.english_term: to have as a wife (male speaker)

indicative_mode.english: I have her as a wife; she is my wife
you have her as a wife; she is your wife
he has her as his wife; she is his wife
you and I have her as a wife; she is our wife

gerundial_mode.english: the one I have as a wife; my wife
the one you have as a wife; your wife
the one he has as a wife; his wife
the one you and I have as a wife; our wife

terms.notes: (ir...) (also extended to mother's brother's wife, wife's sister, sister's son's wife)

terms.english_term: to have as a husband (female speaker)

indicative_mode.english: he has me as a wife; I am his wife
he has you as a wife; you are his wife
he has her as a wife; she is her husband
he has you and me as a wife; he is our husband
he has her and me as a wife; he is our husband

gerundial_mode.english: the one who has me as a wife; my husband
the one who has you as a wife; your husband
the one who has her as a wife; her husband
the one who has you and me as wives; our (IN.DU) husband
the one who has her and me as a wife; our (EX.DU) husband

terms.notes: (ir...) (also extended to husband's brother, sister's husband)

terms.english_term: I have him/her as an in-law
terms.verb_construction: kaakus 'to have as an in-law (male speaking)'
terms.notes: Gerundial Mode: ratutkaaku (the one I have as an in-law; my in-law). Refers to one's wife's parents and brother.

terms.english_term: you have him/her as an in-law
terms.verb_construction: kaakus 'to have as an in-law (male speaking)'
terms.notes: Gerundial Mode: rasutkaaku (the one you have as an in-law; your in-law)

terms.english_term: he has him/her as an in-law
terms.verb_construction: kaakus 'to have as an in-law (male speaking)'
terms.notes: Gerundial Mode: rutkaaku (the one he/she has as an in-law; his/her in-law)

terms.english_term: you and I have him/her as an in-law
terms.verb_construction: kaakus 'to have as an in-law (male speaking)'
terms.notes: Gerundial Mode: racuutkaaku (the one you and I have as an in-law; our (IN.DU) in-law)

terms.english_term: he and I have him/her as an in-law
terms.verb_construction: kaakus 'to have as an in-law (male speaking)'
terms.notes: Gerundial Mode: sirutkaaku (the one he and I have as an in-law; our (EX.DU) in-law)

terms.english_term: he/she has me as a son-in-law/brother-in-law
terms.verb_construction: kaakus 'to have as a son-in-law or brother-in-law (male speaking)'
terms.notes: Gerundial Mode: rikuutkaaku (the one who has me as a son-in-law/brother-in-law; my son-in-law/brother-in-law)

terms.english_term: he/she has you as a son-in-law/brother-in-law
terms.verb_construction: kaakus 'to have as a son-in-law or brother-in-law (male speaking)'
terms.notes: Gerundial Mode: ruutkaaku (the one who has you as a son-in-law/brother-in-law; your son-in-law/brother-in-law)

terms.english_term: he/she has him as a son-in-law/brother-in-law
terms.verb_construction: kaakus 'to have as a son-in-law or brother-in-law (male speaking)'
terms.notes: Gerundial Mode: riitkaaku (the one who has him as a son-in-law/brother-in-law; his brother-in-law/son-in-law)

terms.english_term: he/she has you and me as sons-in-law/brothers-in-law
terms.verb_construction: kaakus 'to have as a son-in-law or brother-in-law (male speaking)'
terms.notes: Gerundial Mode: racakuutkaaku (the one who has you and me as sons-in-law/brothers-in-law)

terms.english_term: he/she has him and me as sons-in-law/brothers-in-law
terms.verb_construction: kaakus 'to have as a son-in-law or brother-in-law (male speaking)'
terms.notes: Gerundial Mode: siriitkaaku (the one who has him and me as sons-in-law/brothers-in-law)

terms.english_term: to have as a female in-law

verb_construction.english_translation: I have her as a female in-law, as a daughter-in-law/sister-in-law
the one I have as a female in-law, as a daughter-in-law/sister-in-law
you have her as a female in-law, as a daughter-in-law/sister-in-law
the one who has you as a female in-law, as a daughter-in-law/sister-in-law
she has her as a female in-law; she is her daughter-in-law/sister-in-law
the one who has her as a female in-law, as a daughter-in-law/sister-in-law

terms.notes: i.e., as a mother-in-law, daughter-in-law, sister-in-law, mother's brother's wife (male and female speaking)

verb_construction.english_translation: she has me as a female in-law; I am her daughter-in-law/sister-in-law
the one who has me as a female in-law; i.e., my mother-in-law/sister-in-law
she has you as a female in-law; you are her daughter-in-law/sister-in-law
the one who has you as a female in-law; your mother-in-law/sister-in-law
she has her as a female in-law; she is her daughter-in-law/sister-in-law
the one who has her as a female in-law; her daughter-in-law/sister-in-law

terms.notes: i.e., as a mother-in-law, daughter-in-law, sister-in-law, mother's brother's wife (female speaking)
not inflected for person; used vocatively and referentially
